title: Skills Framework
cardDescription: The skills framework is the backbone of everything we do. It defines what good looks like and connects learning to real work.
introText: The foundation of everything we do. A single, clear structure that links every role, skill, and learning path - giving dealers and learners a shared language for development.
detailParagraphs: A year and a half ago we rolled out the skills framework and made it the core of what we do. The foundation is solid - and now we know enough from real use to make it genuinely better.

This next phase isn't about adding more for the sake of it. It's about stepping back and asking the harder questions: Is the framework easy enough to use? Does it reflect how work actually looks in dealerships today? Are learners and managers getting enough visibility to act on it?

The initiatives under this pillar cover evaluation, clearer role definitions, better visualization, and ongoing maintenance - all aimed at turning a solid foundation into something that actively helps people understand where they are and where they're going.

focusBoxes.title: Making Skills Easy to Navigate
focusBoxes.description: Today the skills framework exists in several places, making it hard to see how skills connect to learning paths and assessments. We want a single, clear structure that brings everything together in one place. This makes it easier for both our team and dealers to understand requirements, follow progression, and use skills as the real foundation for development.

focusBoxes.title: Granular Role Profiles for Better Development
focusBoxes.description: Today our dealer-facing roles, Technician, Sales, CSR, Parts, and others, are defined at too high a level to be truly useful. We need granular role profiles that capture key tasks, required skills, expected performance levels, and typical learning milestones. These profiles will become the backbone for all future programs, making it possible to tailor learning and assessments in a way that's genuinely relevant to each role.

focusBoxes.title: Does Our Skills Framework Work in Practice?
focusBoxes.description: This initiative is about reviewing the skills framework to ensure it reflects real work and current needs, but it goes beyond checking definitions. We also look at how the framework is actually used and find ways to better support dealers in applying it as intended, so it becomes a genuinely useful tool rather than something that exists only on paper.

focusBoxes.title: Skills Maintenance
focusBoxes.description: This initiative supports the ongoing review and updating of the skills framework, ensuring skills stay relevant, aligned with current work, and reflect changes in products, tools, and dealer needs. To kick off 2026, our focus is on expanding the framework with Warranty and Logistics skills Ã¢â‚¬â€ two areas that have been high on the priority list.

title: Learning Structure & Landscape
cardDescription: A clearer learning landscape that makes structure, navigation, and progression easier to understand.
introText: Learners need a simpler view of the learning landscape. We clarify structure, navigation, and progression so it is easier to understand what exists, what comes next, and how it connects.
detailParagraphs: Today learners can join paths or take lessons, but the overall structure is not always clear. This creates uncertainty around progression, priorities, and what different content is for.
A more intentional learning landscape groups related assets into meaningful structures and makes it easier for both learners and managers to navigate development.

focusBoxes.title: A Learner Journey That Makes Sense
focusBoxes.description: Learners need a clear way to see where they are and what comes next. We will make the journey in Veida easier to navigate for both new and experienced users. With logical steps, better links to skills, and a structure that helps people understand their progress and what to focus on next.

focusBoxes.title: Learning That Fits Together, Not Just Side by Side
focusBoxes.description: Today learning in Veida can feel like disconnected pieces. We will build a program-based structure that groups related learning into a coherent whole. For example, a Workshop Technician program covering onboarding, maintenance, and repair. This gives learners a clearer sense of direction and makes development feel purposeful.

focusBoxes.title: Getting the Most Out of Veida
focusBoxes.description: We want to make sure we're using Veida in the best possible way - not just today, but with an eye on what's coming. This initiative looks at how we structure content using courses, channels, and other platform features, and investigates how Valamis' future development roadmap will affect us. The goal is to ensure our setup is both effective now and well-positioned for what's next.

title: Assessment & Validation
cardDescription: Confirming that learning has translated into real capability, and that our assessments and assessors are equipped to do that reliably.
introText: Assessment and validation is how we confirm that learning has translated into real capability. This pillar covers two things: understanding where learners are before they start, and making sure the assessments and assessors we already have are working as well as they should.
detailParagraphs: Without a clear picture of current capability, learners can end up in training that doesn't match where they actually are. And even well-designed learning falls short if the assessments measuring it aren't relevant or if assessors don't feel fully equipped to carry them out. Getting both right, a strong starting point and a reliable validation process, is what turns learning into something we can stand behind with confidence.

focusBoxes.title: Pre-assessment to identify current level and skill gaps
focusBoxes.description: Pre-assessment gives learners and managers a clear view of current skill levels before training or validation begins. Instead of guessing at readiness, both sides get concrete information: what's already in place, where the gaps are, and what learning or practice makes most sense as a next step. Ideally, pre-assessment results feed directly into Veida Ã¢â‚¬â€ pointing learners toward the most relevant content and making the path to validation more efficient and targeted.

focusBoxes.title: Are Our Assessments and Assessors Fit for Purpose?
focusBoxes.description: We have built a solid base of assessments Ã¢â‚¬â€ now it's time to look critically at how well they're working. This initiative evaluates the quality and relevance of existing assessments and explores whether anything needs to be updated or improved. It also takes a closer look at the assessor role: do assessors have the training they need, and do they feel confident in carrying out assessments consistently and fairly?

title: Manager Involvement
cardDescription: Managers are the link between learning and real performance. We want to make that role easier to fulfil.
introText: Managers need a clearer role and better tools to support their team's development, and the visibility to act on it.
detailParagraphs: Learning doesn't end when a lesson is completed. What happens back in the workshop or on the floor is where skills are either applied and developed further, or forgotten. Managers are the people best placed to make that transfer happen, but only if they know what to look for and feel equipped to act on it. Making manager involvement a priority in 2026 is about closing that gap and making sure development doesn't stop at the screen.

title: Social Learning
cardDescription: Peer exchange, mentoring, and team reflection that turn learning into practice.
introText: Social learning happens through discussing, observing, sharing experiences, and solving problems together. It strengthens skill development beyond individual lessons.
detailParagraphs: Much of what people learn at work doesn't come from a course, it comes from a colleague, a conversation, or watching someone more experienced handle a situation. When we create the right conditions for that kind of exchange, learning becomes a natural part of daily work rather than something that happens separately from it.

Supporting mentoring, enabling peer interaction, and connecting dealer trainers with each other and with our regional trainers means that knowledge doesn't stay siloed. It spreads, gets applied, and builds confidence across the network.

focusBoxes.title: Mentoring Support
focusBoxes.description: Having a mentor makes a real difference. learners feel more confident and apply new skills more effectively in their daily work. We will develop a simple mentoring approach and share best practices with dealers, offered as a practical option to strengthen how learning transfers into real performance.

focusBoxes.title: How Do We Best Support Social Learning in Dealerships?
focusBoxes.description: People learn a great deal from each other, but the conditions for that don't always exist naturally. This initiative investigates how we can actively support social learning in dealerships, whether through tools in Veida, structured activities, or other forms of peer interaction. So that knowledge sharing becomes a normal part of how teams develop together.

focusBoxes.title: A Space for Dealer Trainers to Connect and Grow
focusBoxes.description: We already have a dealer trainer network. This initiative builds on it. By creating a dedicated forum, we give trainers a live space to ask questions, share experiences, and receive new material and practical tips directly from our regional trainers. It strengthens the connections that already exist and makes the network more useful in everyday work.

title: Learning Support
cardDescription: AI assistance, smart nudging, and timely prompts that keep learning on track.
introText: Good learning support meets people where they are - in the flow of their work, at the right moment.
detailParagraphs: Completing a lesson is only part of the picture. Without the right support around it - timely prompts, practical guidance, and someone to turn to when questions come up - learning easily loses momentum.
This pillar is about making sure learners, managers, and trainers all have what they need, when they need it, to keep development moving forward.

focusBoxes.title: Veida AI Support / Agent
focusBoxes.description: A Veida's AI agent that would give users quick, targeted help directly inside the platform - no extra steps, no waiting. Learners can get answers to common questions, guidance on where to start, or links to relevant content. Assessors get support with criteria and checklists, and managers get quick snapshots of team progress. It's practical assistance built into the flow of learning.

focusBoxes.title: Nudging and pacing prompts between modules
focusBoxes.description: Many learners complete digital modules either very late or with long gaps between sessions, making it harder to retain and apply what they've learned. This initiative introduces timely prompts and practice opportunities to encourage a more consistent pace — and gives managers simple reminders to help them follow up and keep learning on track in daily work.

focusBoxes.title: Integrate Learning into Workflows
focusBoxes.description: Learning sticks best when it happens close to the work itself. This initiative explores how we can embed guidance, practice, and performance support into daily tasks and tools - reducing the gap between learning something and using it, and making development a natural part of how people work rather than a separate activity.

title: Structure & Processes
cardDescription: Clear ownership, workflows, and routines that make the learning ecosystem maintainable.
introText: Good learning doesn't run itself. Behind every program and piece of content there needs to be a clear structure - so we know what we have, why it exists, and how to keep it relevant.
detailParagraphs: As our learning offering grows, so does the need for clarity around what we're building and how. Without defined processes and dedicated spaces for development, content becomes inconsistent and hard to maintain.

These two initiatives are about making sure we have the right foundations in place, both for the formal programs we run and for the additional content that supports curious learners who want to go further.

focusBoxes.title: Are Our Technical Programs Fit for Purpose?
focusBoxes.description: Our technical programs form the core of dealer learning, but are they sufficient in their current form? This initiative takes a structured look at whether existing programs meet the needs of learners today, and whether there are gaps that could be addressed through additional events, practical labs, or other formats. The goal is programs that are thorough, relevant, and genuinely prepare people for the work they do.

focusBoxes.title: A Dedicated Space for Additional Learning Content
focusBoxes.description: Not all learning fits neatly into a formal path. And that's a good thing. This initiative creates a defined space and process for developing additional mlearning content aimed at the curious learner who wants to go beyond the basics. By giving this content a clear home and a structured development process, we make it easier to produce, maintain, and discover, turning curiosity into real development.
